en.extract:
The Atherton scrubwren (Sericornis keri) is a bird species. Placed in the family Pardalotidae in the Sibley-Ahlquist taxonomy, this has met with opposition and indeed is now known to be wrong; they rather belong to the independent family Acanthizidae.
It is endemic to Queensland (south-eastern coasts of Cape York Peninsula). Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''Atherton scrubwren''' ('''''Sericornis keri''''') is a [[bird]] [[species]]. Placed in the [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Pardalotidae]] in the [[Sibley-Ahlquist taxonomy]], this has met with opposition and indeed is now known to be wrong; they rather belong to the independent family [[Acanthizidae]].

It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Queensland]] (south-eastern coasts of [[Cape York Peninsula]]). Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
